TEXT.citation: Wikipedia contributors. (2026). Sakizaya Wikipedia. Wikimedia Foundation. Retrieved 2026-06-09, from https://szy.wikipedia.org/
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy, title = {Sakizaya Wikipedia}, author = {{Wikipedia contributors}}, year = {2026}, publisher = {Wikimedia Foundation}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/}, urldate = {2026-06-09} }

S.FORM: == salahebal == ÂêëÊó•Ëëµ mahiza ku cilal, u balu sa midudu ku cilal. taneng mukan, taneng pasapayu'. kapah miazih. yadah ku ngangan. u siwkay nu nipaluma u sanek nu nipaluma malaheci tu imelang pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u zuma a cidekay a kamu, saungay misapayu' a cidekay u sulit nu zuma a kamu-Hulam atu Amilika ÂêëÊó•Ëëµ(Â≠∏Âêç:Helianthus annuus) :jiÃçt-th√¢u-hue) , . Helianthus annuus, the common sunflower, is a large annual forb of the genus Helianthus grown as a crop for its edible oil and edible fruits . This sunflower species is also used as bird food, as livestock forage (as a meal or a silageplant), and in some industrial applications. The plant was first domesticated in the Americas. Wild Helianthus annuus is a widely branched annual plant with many flower heads. The domestic sunflower, however, often possesses only a single large inflorescence atop an unbranched stem. The name sunflower may derive from the flower's head's shape, which resembles the sun, or from the impression that the blooming plant appears to slowly turn its flower towards the sun as the latter moves across the sky on a daily basis. Sunflower seeds were brought to Europe from the Americas in the 16th century, where, along with sunflower oil, they became a widespread cooking ingredient.
S.PHON: == sar~…æaƒß…ôbar~…æ == *** maƒßiza ku ¶ir~…æar~…æ, u bar~…æu sa midudu ku ¶ir~…æar~…æ. tan…ô≈ã mukan, tan…ô≈ã pasapaju î~ °. kapaƒß miaziƒß. jadaƒß ku ≈ãa≈ãan. u siwkaj nu nipar~…æuma u san…ôk nu nipar~…æuma mar~…æaƒß…ô ¶i tu im…ôr~…æa≈ã pasubana i ¶a ¶udadan a r~…æa ¶ur~…æ u zuma a ¶id…ôkaj a kamu, sau≈ãaj misapaju î~ ° a ¶id…ôkaj u sur~…æit nu zuma a kamu-ƒßur~…æam atu amir~…æika ***(**:ƒß…ôr~…æiantƒßus annuus) :ji*t-tƒß*u-ƒßu…ô) , . ƒß…ôr~…æiantƒßus annuus, tƒß…ô ¶ommon sunfr~…æow…ôr, is a r~…æarg…ô annuar~…æ forb of tƒß…ô g…ônus ƒß…ôr~…æiantƒßus grown as a ¶rop for its …ôdibr~…æ…ô oir~…æ and …ôdibr~…æ…ô fruits . tƒßis sunfr~…æow…ôr sp…ô ¶i…ôs is ar~…æso us…ôd as bird food, as r~…æi*…ôsto ¶k forag…ô (as a m…ôar~…æ or a sir~…æag…ôpr~…æant), and in som…ô industriar~…æ appr~…æi ¶ations. tƒß…ô pr~…æant was first dom…ôsti ¶at…ôd in tƒß…ô am…ôri ¶as. wir~…æd ƒß…ôr~…æiantƒßus annuus is a wid…ôr~…æj bran ¶ƒß…ôd annuar~…æ pr~…æant witƒß manj fr~…æow…ôr ƒß…ôads. tƒß…ô dom…ôsti ¶ sunfr~…æow…ôr, ƒßow…ô*…ôr, oft…ôn poss…ôss…ôs onr~…æj a si≈ãr~…æ…ô r~…æarg…ô infr~…æor…ôs ¶…ôn ¶…ô atop an unbran ¶ƒß…ôd st…ôm. tƒß…ô nam…ô sunfr~…æow…ôr maj d…ôri*…ô from tƒß…ô fr~…æow…ôr î~ °s ƒß…ôad î~ °s sƒßap…ô, wƒßi ¶ƒß r…ôs…ômbr~…æ…ôs tƒß…ô sun, or from tƒß…ô impr…ôssion tƒßat tƒß…ô br~…æoomi≈ã pr~…æant app…ôars to sr~…æowr~…æj turn its fr~…æow…ôr towards tƒß…ô sun as tƒß…ô r~…æatt…ôr mo*…ôs a ¶ross tƒß…ô skj on a dair~…æj basis. sunfr~…æow…ôr s…ô…ôds w…ôr…ô brougƒßt to …ôurop…ô from tƒß…ô am…ôri ¶as in tƒß…ô **tƒß ¶…ônturj, wƒß…ôr…ô, ar~…æo≈ã witƒß sunfr~…æow…ôr oir~…æ, tƒß…ô b…ô ¶am…ô a wid…ôspr…ôad ¶ooki≈ã i≈ãr…ôdi…ônt.
S.FORM: == salahebal == ÂêëÊó•Ëëµ mahiza ku cilal, u balu sa midudu ku cilal. taneng mukan, taneng pasapayu'. kapah miazih. yadah ku ngangan. u siwkay nu nipaluma u sanek nu nipaluma malaheci tu imelang pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u zuma a cidekay a kamu, saungay misapayu' a cidekay u sulit nu zuma a kamu-Hulam atu Amilika ÂêëÊó•Ëëµ(Â≠∏Âêç:Helianthus annuus) :jiÃçt-th√¢u-hue) , . Helianthus annuus, the common sunflower, is a large annual forb of the genus Helianthus grown as a crop for its edible oil and edible fruits . This sunflower species is also used as bird food, as livestock forage (as a meal or a silageplant), and in some industrial applications. The plant was first domesticated in the Americas. Wild Helianthus annuus is a widely branched annual plant with many flower heads. The domestic sunflower, however, often possesses only a single large inflorescence atop an unbranched stem. The name sunflower may derive from the flower's head's shape, which resembles the sun, or from the impression that the blooming plant appears to slowly turn its flower towards the sun as the latter moves across the sky on a daily basis. Sunflower seeds were brought to Europe from the Americas in the 16th century, where, along with sunflower oil, they became a widespread cooking ingredient.
S.PHON: == sar~…æaƒß…ôbar~…æ == *** maƒßiza ku ¶ir~…æar~…æ, u bar~…æu sa midudu ku ¶ir~…æar~…æ. tan…ô≈ã mukan, tan…ô≈ã pasapaju î~ °. kapaƒß miaziƒß. jadaƒß ku ≈ãa≈ãan. u siwkaj nu nipar~…æuma u san…ôk nu nipar~…æuma mar~…æaƒß…ô ¶i tu im…ôr~…æa≈ã pasubana i ¶a ¶udadan a r~…æa ¶ur~…æ u zuma a ¶id…ôkaj a kamu, sau≈ãaj misapaju î~ ° a ¶id…ôkaj u sur~…æit nu zuma a kamu-ƒßur~…æam atu amir~…æika ***(**:ƒß…ôr~…æiantƒßus annuus) :ji*t-tƒß*u-ƒßu…ô) , . ƒß…ôr~…æiantƒßus annuus, tƒß…ô ¶ommon sunfr~…æow…ôr, is a r~…æarg…ô annuar~…æ forb of tƒß…ô g…ônus ƒß…ôr~…æiantƒßus grown as a ¶rop for its …ôdibr~…æ…ô oir~…æ and …ôdibr~…æ…ô fruits . tƒßis sunfr~…æow…ôr sp…ô ¶i…ôs is ar~…æso us…ôd as bird food, as r~…æi*…ôsto ¶k forag…ô (as a m…ôar~…æ or a sir~…æag…ôpr~…æant), and in som…ô industriar~…æ appr~…æi ¶ations. tƒß…ô pr~…æant was first dom…ôsti ¶at…ôd in tƒß…ô am…ôri ¶as. wir~…æd ƒß…ôr~…æiantƒßus annuus is a wid…ôr~…æj bran ¶ƒß…ôd annuar~…æ pr~…æant witƒß manj fr~…æow…ôr ƒß…ôads. tƒß…ô dom…ôsti ¶ sunfr~…æow…ôr, ƒßow…ô*…ôr, oft…ôn poss…ôss…ôs onr~…æj a si≈ãr~…æ…ô r~…æarg…ô infr~…æor…ôs ¶…ôn ¶…ô atop an unbran ¶ƒß…ôd st…ôm. tƒß…ô nam…ô sunfr~…æow…ôr maj d…ôri*…ô from tƒß…ô fr~…æow…ôr î~ °s ƒß…ôad î~ °s sƒßap…ô, wƒßi ¶ƒß r…ôs…ômbr~…æ…ôs tƒß…ô sun, or from tƒß…ô impr…ôssion tƒßat tƒß…ô br~…æoomi≈ã pr~…æant app…ôars to sr~…æowr~…æj turn its fr~…æow…ôr towards tƒß…ô sun as tƒß…ô r~…æatt…ôr mo*…ôs a ¶ross tƒß…ô skj on a dair~…æj basis. sunfr~…æow…ôr s…ô…ôds w…ôr…ô brougƒßt to …ôurop…ô from tƒß…ô am…ôri ¶as in tƒß…ô **tƒß ¶…ônturj, wƒß…ôr…ô, ar~…æo≈ã witƒß sunfr~…æow…ôr oir~…æ, tƒß…ô b…ô ¶am…ô a wid…ôspr…ôad ¶ooki≈ã i≈ãr…ôdi…ônt.
